input_row.title: Ensure incoming client certificates are enabled and required (if in use)
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Incoming client certificates, set Client certificate mode to Required. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each function app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to enable and require incoming client certificates: az resource update --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function- app-name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name> --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" --set properties.clientCertEnabled=true --set properties.clientCertMode=Required Default Value: By default, incoming client certificates are disabled. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- identity-management#im-4-authenticate-server-and-services 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/app-service/app-service-web-configure- tls-mutual-auth 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/resource CIS Controls:
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- identity-management#im-4-authenticate-server-and-services 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/app-service/app-service-web-configure- tls-mutual-auth 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/resource CIS Controls: Controls Version Control IG 1 IG 2 IG 3 v8 3.3 Configure Data Access Control Lists Configure data access control lists based on a user’s need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications. ● ● ● v7 14.6 Protect Information through Access Control Lists Protect all information stored on systems with file system, network share, claims, application, or database specific access control lists. These controls will enforce the principle that only authorized individuals should have access to the information based on their need to access the information as a part of their responsibilities. ● ● ●

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The remediation steps provided specify Azure CLI commands that can be executed to enable and require incoming client certificates, indicating that this control can indeed be validated using Azure APIs.
- The absence of the audit field is mitigated by the presence of the remediation instructions, which inherently support automated checks through the command line.
- Given the alignment of the control with Azure resource properties and the ability to query these properties, the decision is clear.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_function_app_incoming_client_certificates_required

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program would use the Azure CLI command provided in the remediation section:
  - For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the command:
    ```
    az resource update --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name> --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" --set properties.clientCertEnabled=true --set properties.clientCertMode=Required
    ```
- Specific resources to enumerate include:
  - Azure Function Apps and their deployment slots within the specified resource groups.
- The configuration properties/settings to validate include:
  - `properties.clientCertEnabled` should be `true`.
  - `properties.clientCertMode` should be `Required`.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** If both `clientCertEnabled` is `true` and `clientCertMode` is `Required`, the compliance check passes.
  - **Fail:** If either property does not meet the expected value, the compliance check fails.
- Example Azure CLI command to check existing settings for a deployment slot:
  ```
  az resource show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name> --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" --query "properties.{clientCertEnabled:clientCertEnabled, clientCertMode:clientCertMode}"
  ```
